evals.prompt: I have an APDL script called thermal_model.inp and a MAPDL instance already running on port 50060. How do I send that script to the running instance from my terminal?
evals.expected_output: A command using pymapdl exec --file thermal_model.inp --port 50060, with explanation that exec connects to an existing instance

assertions.description: Uses pymapdl exec (not pymapdl run or other command)
Includes --file flag with the script name
Includes --port 50060 to target the correct instance
Explains that exec connects to an already-running instance

evals.prompt: I need to start MAPDL, create a simple block element, mesh it, apply constraints and loads, solve, and then shut everything down. All from the command line, no Python scripting. Walk me through the exact commands.
evals.expected_output: A step-by-step sequence: pymapdl start, then multiple pymapdl exec -c calls for /prep7, ET, BLOCK, VMESH, boundary conditions, /solu, SOLVE, and finally pymapdl stop

assertions.description: Uses pymapdl exec with -c flags for APDL commands
Commands are in correct order: start before exec before stop
assertions.check: start appears before exec which appears before stop

evals.prompt: What MAPDL instances are running on my machine right now? I want to see the PID and port for each one, but only the main instances, not child processes.
evals.expected_output: The pymapdl list -i command, with explanation of the -i flag filtering to instances only and showing PID/port columns

assertions.description: Includes -i or --instances flag to filter to main instances
Explains that -i filters out child processes
assertions.check: mentions filtering child processes or showing only instances
